novel_features.name: Revenue snapshot
novel_features.description: Point-in-time revenue rollup combining LS's denormalized 30-day/lifetime store counters with refund-adjusted net from local orders.
novel_features.rationale: No single Lemon Squeezy endpoint returns refund-adjusted net alongside the store's pre-rolled 30-day counters — requires joining synced stores with synced orders.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you want one number for 'how is the store doing right now' without walking every order through the API.

novel_features.name: MRR trend
novel_features.description: Weekly MRR over a sliding window, classified as new / renewal / refunded with a week-over-week net delta.
novel_features.rationale: Time-series MRR with movement classification requires cross-table SQL on locally synced subscriptions and subscription-invoices — the LS API only exposes raw lists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a raw `list-subscriptions` call when you need to show how MRR moved week-over-week, not just where it stands today.

novel_features.name: Churn watch
novel_features.description: Lists subscriptions that flipped to past_due/unpaid/cancelled/expired in a window, with customer email and dollar exposure per row.
novel_features.rationale: Requires diffing the current snapshot against a prior-sync snapshot of subscription status — a pure local SQLite operation that no API call exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this on Monday-morning sweeps to see what changed since Friday — beats clicking through the dashboard's status filters.

novel_features.name: Dunning alert
novel_features.description: Lists subscription-invoices with status=failed whose parent subscription is still active or past_due — the recoverable window.
novel_features.rationale: Dunning is implicit in Lemon Squeezy's state machine (no separate resource) — requires joining invoices to subscriptions locally with a status filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to find subs whose latest renewal failed but who haven't churned yet — the window where a Slack ping or grace email recovers revenue.

novel_features.name: License-key roll-up
novel_features.description: Per-variant and per-key activation statistics joined across license-keys, license-key-instances, and variants.
novel_features.rationale: LS exposes the keys and instances as separate endpoints with no aggregate — the rollup is a SQLite triple-join that hits the same data in one pass.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need to surface piracy-shaped seat distributions or just answer 'how many keys are active per variant'.

novel_features.name: Refund cascade
novel_features.description: Given a refunded order ID, walks order → order-items → license-keys → instances, then optionally disables the keys via --apply.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a Lemon-Squeezy-specific 4-endpoint orchestration with mutation gating — no SDK exposes this as one call, and no MCP does the cascade.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this after a refund hits, to make sure the buyer's license keys actually get disabled instead of staying active.

novel_features.name: Campaign watch
novel_features.description: Per discount code: redemptions used vs cap, redemption velocity over last 24h, projected sellout time at current pace (needs >=6h of redemption activity to stabilise).
novel_features.rationale: Sellout pace requires reading local redemption timestamps and computing velocity — no LS endpoint surfaces pace or projected sellout.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this during a capped sale (Founding-Member tier, Early Access) to know when a tier will sell out so you can expire the code before overselling.

novel_features.name: Webhook audit
novel_features.description: Cross-store webhook coverage matrix grouped by URL host, flagging stale destinations (localhost, ngrok, *.test, *.local).
novel_features.rationale: LS's dashboard is per-store — there's no cross-store grouping view, and stale-host detection isn't anywhere in the official surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch orphaned webhooks pointing at last week's ngrok URL before they cause a missed event in production.

narrative.headline: Every Lemon Squeezy resource, plus a local SQLite mirror that surfaces MRR, churn, license seats, and discount-campaign pace no other LS tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Lemon Squeezy's official SDKs are libraries, not CLIs, and the dashboard is a click-walk per store. This CLI mirrors all 19 LS resources to local SQLite with offline FTS5 and cross-entity SQL, then layers transcendence commands — `revenue-snapshot`, `mrr-trend`, `churn-watch`, `dunning-alert`, `license-rollup`, `refund-cascade`, `campaign-watch`, `webhook-audit` — that combine entities no single endpoint returns. Built for indie SaaS founders and license-key sellers who live in the LS state machine.
narrative.auth_narrative: Lemon Squeezy uses HTTP Bearer auth. Create an API key at https://app.lemonsqueezy.com/settings/api, then export `LEMONSQUEEZY_API_KEY=<your-key>`. Verify with `lemonsqueezy-pp-cli doctor`.

quickstart.comment: Verify the binary loads and shows expected env vars before hitting the API.
Pull the core surface into local SQLite so the transcendence commands have data to work with.
Get a one-number rollup of 30-day + lifetime revenue, refund-adjusted from local orders.
List subscriptions that flipped into past_due/unpaid/cancelled in the last 7 days with dollar exposure.

troubleshoots.fix: Export `LEMONSQUEEZY_API_KEY=<your-key>` in your shell (or persist in ~/.zshenv for non-interactive subprocesses).
The API key is invalid or revoked. Generate a fresh one at app.lemonsqueezy.com/settings/api.
Lemon Squeezy rate-limits at 300 req/min per IP. Rerun after the `Retry-After` window or narrow `sync --resources` to a smaller set.
Run `sync --resources subscriptions,subscription-invoices --full` first — these commands read the local mirror, not the live API.
The projection needs at least 6 hours of redemption history. Resync `discount-redemptions` and rerun once redemptions exist in the window.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when running ongoing Lemon Squeezy operations — Monday-morning revenue/churn sweeps, post-refund license-key disables, live capacity tracking during a capped sale, or webhook coverage audits across multiple stores. The cross-entity SQL and transcendence commands are the differentiator. For one-shot dashboard tasks (creating one product, editing a single discount) the LS web UI is still faster.
anti_triggers: Building a custom checkout form in a web app — use the official `lemonsqueezy.js` JavaScript SDK (the CLI is for ops, not embedded billing).
Receiving webhook payloads in production — this CLI can replay and audit webhooks, but the actual HTTP server lives in your application.
Tax/VAT reporting — Lemon Squeezy is merchant-of-record and ships its own tax docs in the dashboard; this CLI doesn't replicate them.
Customer-facing license validation in your desktop app — call the LS license API directly from the app, not via this CLI.

recipes.title: Monday-morning revenue + churn sweep
recipes.command: lemonsqueezy-pp-cli sync --resources stores,subscriptions,subscription-invoices,orders --since 14d && lemonsqueezy-pp-cli revenue-snapshot --json && lemonsqueezy-pp-cli churn-watch --since 7d --json
recipes.explanation: Refreshes the local mirror, then prints the revenue rollup and the past-week churn delta — the ritual that replaces the dashboard click-walk.

recipes.title: Find recoverable failed renewals
recipes.explanation: Lists every failed invoice whose subscription is still active or past_due — the dollar-recoverable window before the customer churns.

recipes.title: Refund cascade for an order
recipes.explanation: Walks the order → order-items → license-keys chain and previews which keys would be disabled. Add --apply on the rerun to actually disable them (the command refuses --apply unless LS itself reports the order as refunded).

recipes.title: Track a capped Founding-Member sale
recipes.command: lemonsqueezy-pp-cli sync --resources discounts,discount-redemptions && lemonsqueezy-pp-cli campaign-watch FOUNDING-LIFETIME FOUNDING-2YR FOUNDING-1YR --json
recipes.explanation: Live capacity + redemption velocity per tier with sellout projection — run hourly during a launch to expire codes before overselling.

recipes.title: Webhook coverage audit
recipes.command: lemonsqueezy-pp-cli sync --resources webhooks && lemonsqueezy-pp-cli webhook-audit --json --select hosts.url,hosts.stale,hosts.event_count
recipes.explanation: Groups every webhook across every store by URL host, flags localhost/ngrok/*.test destinations so orphans don't break production.

trigger_phrases: disable license keys for refunded order
